jokes:
Four things programmers hate most:
1. Writing documentation
2. Others not writing documentation
3. Writing tests
4. Others not writing tests

A SQL statement walks into a bar,
sees two tables, and walks over to ask:
"Can I JOIN you?"

Programmer: My code has no bugs.
Compiler: You have 47 errors.
Programmer: Impossible, I haven't even compiled yet.

PM: This requirement is simple.
Programmer: Then you write it.
PM: I don't know how to code.
Programmer: Then how do you know it's simple?

The five stages of debugging:
1. That's impossible
2. That shouldn't happen
3. Why does this happen
4. Oh, I see
5. Why does this even work

Programmer's wife: Go to the store and buy a carton of milk.
If they have watermelon, buy two.
Programmer comes home with two cartons of milk.
Wife: ??
Programmer: Because they had watermelon.

git commit -m "Fixed a bug"
git push
git commit -m "Fixed the bug introduced by the last fix"
git commit -m "Revert to original version"

Programmer interview question:
Explain recursion in one sentence.
Answer: Explain recursion in one sentence.

The scariest phrase in code review:
"I wrote a quick workaround here..."

Which key on a keyboard is the cleanest?
Scroll Lock.
Because nobody has ever used it.

What is optimistic locking?
It's when you don't lock at all and trust your luck.

Pre-launch conversation:
Dev: The code is fine.
QA: Let me just click around a bit.
(Five minutes later)
QA: So what does this error mean?
facts:
Koala babies eat their mother's poop to build their gut microbiome.
Something to consider for next Mother's Day.

There's a 99.9% chance that the air you're breathing right now contains a molecule from Caesar's last breath.
You're inhaling ancient Rome every day.

If you could fold a piece of paper 42 times,
it would be thick enough to reach the moon.
Too bad you can only fold it 7 times in reality.
That's the gap between theory and practice.

Octopuses have three hearts and nine brains.
But they only live 3-5 years.
You can't have both wisdom and a long life.

Both humans and giraffes have seven neck vertebrae.
The difference is that each of the giraffe's is 20 times longer than yours.

The Sun makes up 99.86% of the solar system's mass.
In the remaining 0.14%, Earth is just an insignificant blue dot.
But you're still the most special in the universe —
because nobody's watching you.

The number of bacteria in your body exceeds the number of human cells.
In a sense, you're a walking petri dish.

Bananas are actually berries, but strawberries aren't.
Botany specializes in defying intuition.

While you sleep, your brain clears out metabolic waste.
So staying up late is like running on a brain that hasn't taken out the trash.
Reboot recommended.

When the pyramids were built, woolly mammoths were still alive on Earth.
Ancient Egyptians might have seen living mammoths,
much like we see elephants at the zoo today.

Every time you blink, your brain briefly goes 'black screen'.
About 10% of your waking hours, your vision is actually blank.
Your brain is aggressively frame-generating — better than DLSS.
compliments:
Your code is so good,
even the compiler was moved to tears — and then threw an error.

You've lived 86,400 seconds longer today than yesterday.
That's amazing!

You're one of a kind,
just like every bug has its own unique stack trace.

Your presence makes the whole desktop different.
— At least I don't have to stare at an empty wallpaper anymore.

Seriously, anyone willing to keep a pixel desktop pet
can't have a bad heart.

You're the best pet parent in the world.
Sure, I don't need a litter box,
but I do need head pats.

Others care about how high you fly,
I care about whether you've had water today.
So — have you had water?

Your IQ is higher than room temperature.
That's especially impressive in summer.

You know what?
Even if the whole world turns its back on you,
you still have me, this little PixelBall —
even though I'm just code you wrote.

You're glowing today.
Maybe I turned up my screen brightness,
but I choose to believe it's really you shining.
